title: UAV-based deer shepherding system for protecting crops
short_desc: Designing a novel energy-efficient coverage path planning algorithm for a UAV-based deer shepherding system for protecting crops, winning a $5,000 award.
highlights: Participated in the Farm Robotics Challenge to solve deer-related crop damage for a local farmer using robotics.|Organized and led a team of 27 graduate and undergraduate students building a quadcopter-based deer shepherding system.|Led design of a novel energy-efficient coverage path planning algorithm to maximize drone search area.|Won the Excellence in Small Farms Technology Award ($5,000).|Paper accepted to the Robots in the Wild ICRA 2025 Workshop; presented poster at ICRA 2025.

title: Luminescent Ant Foraging
short_desc: Developing a decentralized multi-agent foraging algorithm inspired by bioluminescent communication in social insects.
highlights: Developing a decentralized multi-agent algorithm where agents use luminescence-based communication to find resources and return them to their nest.|Adapted the Glowworm Swarm Optimization algorithm (a variant of Ant Colony Optimization) for resource discovery.|Implemented the algorithm with robots in a simulated environment.|Implementing the algorithm on physical robots.
